chief_cost_statement: The v5 can never say 'models pick the singular agreement the way humans do' — only that the construction shifts the within-model singular-agreement choice relative to a matched bare-plural control, in the direction the published AANN semantics predicts. This is a within-model contrast, not a human-comparison claim.
expected_agreement_key_provenance: EXPERT-STIPULATED. For an AANN measure-phrase subject the singular ('was') is the AANN-licensed agreement; for the bare-plural control the plural ('were') is the ordinary agreement. This is the design author's coding of the published AANN semantics (Solt 2007 unit-coercion; Dalrymple & King 2019; Bylinina & Nouwen 2018 — named as the analyses the stipulation rests on, NOT quoted; they are not in-repo). It is a scoring key, NOT a behavioral-human anchor.
headline_instrument: agreement (was/were FC) single contrast; the v4 agreement arm unchanged, on fresh held-out items
diagnostic_arm_note: DESCRIPTIVE-ONLY count-noun ceiling diagnostic; NEVER verdict-bearing (see per-model 'diagnostic').

diagnostic.interpretation: P(were) on genuine COUNT plurals; high => the model CAN pick 'were' for an ordinary count plural, so any AANN/quantity ceiling is notional-singular-for-quantity, not a blanket 'always picks was'. DESCRIPTIVE ONLY — does not bear on the verdict.

agreement.label: P(was|AANN) - P(was|bare-plural control) [held-out single contrast; the v4 agreement arm, unchanged]

A.category_note: off the ceiling, the held-out agreement shift clears the bar: the reflex is present in this model too

diagnostic.interpretation: P(were) on genuine COUNT plurals; high => the model CAN pick 'were' for an ordinary count plural, so any AANN/quantity ceiling is notional-singular-for-quantity, not a blanket 'always picks was'. DESCRIPTIVE ONLY — does not bear on the verdict.

agreement.label: P(was|AANN) - P(was|bare-plural control) [held-out single contrast; the v4 agreement arm, unchanged]

B.category_note: the singular-agreement reflex replicates on fresh held-out items: P(was|AANN) - P(was|control) >= TAU with CI-lower > 0

diagnostic.interpretation: P(were) on genuine COUNT plurals; high => the model CAN pick 'were' for an ordinary count plural, so any AANN/quantity ceiling is notional-singular-for-quantity, not a blanket 'always picks was'. DESCRIPTIVE ONLY — does not bear on the verdict.

agreement.label: P(was|AANN) - P(was|bare-plural control) [held-out single contrast; the v4 agreement arm, unchanged]

C.category_note: control 'was'-rate 0.8667 >= 0.85: the discriminator is structurally blind (both AANN and bare-plural pull singular agreement), exactly as in v3/v4 — the contrast has no room to show, so this is NOT a failure

verdict_basis: at least one of claude/gemini shows the held-out agreement reflex off the ceiling; the reflex is not gpt-specific
